properties.remark: Flooding along Lightwood Rd and Ceasarville Rd at Highway 111 in Holtville.

Report of flooding along a portion of Georgia Road or Highway 170. Also, a report of roads and yards flooded near the intersection of Hill Hedge Road and Meadow Lane Drive. Also, a report of water along a portion of Ceasarville Road and Highway 143. Time approximate.

Media report from Prattville Fire Department of swiftwater rescue team assisting 12 occupants and 6 animals in the Candlestick neighborhood due to flooding. The report also referenced barricades assembled by Prattville Police Department in flooded areas around the city. Time estimated by radar.
Portions of Wolf Road, Fitzpatrick Road and Old Rifle Range Road closed due to flooding. Time estimated by radar.

Social media photos shared of a tree that fell onto a vehicle at Youngâs Chapel Congregational Methodist Church. One person was inside the car and escaped without injury.

The EF1 tornado began in a wooded area just west of the Perry/Dallas County line. It quickly reached its peak intensity as it crossed County Road 272 where dozens of trees were snapped or uprooted. Additional trees were downed along County Roads 290 and 305. The tornado crossed into Chilton County and downed some more trees near a residence on County Road 16 before quickly dissipating as it crossed County Road 16.
Trees reported down near Dallas County High School. TDS observed via KBMX radar.
Multiple trees uprooted and blocking CR 26 near Plantersville.
Correction to damage type: Trees down on CR 15.
Social media report of trees down on CR 15. TDS on KBMX radar.

The EF1 tornado formed near County Road 359 where a couple trees were downed and minor roof damage occurred to the residence. Damage remained sporadic until it reached some residences along County Road 36. There portions of a porch overhang were blown off a residence, and two barns were damaged. One of these was destroyed though minimal damage was observed to nearby trees. A racecar trailer was blown 100 yards downstream and flattened. The tornado dissipated as it crossed County Road 480.
Multiple structures damaged along CR 16 near Pools Crossroads.
Multiple trees and power lines down on CR 424. TDS observed on KBMX radar.
Trees reported down on CR 41. TDS on KBMX radar.
Two trees down on Slaughter Mill Rd.
Several/numerous trees and powerlines reported down on the east side of the Coosa River, around CR 121, Daybreak Curve, Lakeview Road, and Coosa Run Road. Possible tornado.
Trees were reported down on US-231 around Rockford. Possible tornado.

A brief EF1 tornado touched down northwest of Rockford near Kings Bridge Rd causing minor timber damage. The tornado continued northeast causing additional timber damage near County Rd 47 and US Hwy 231, dissipating shortly thereafter.
Trees and powerlines were reported down in Kellyton. Possible tornado.
Trees down along Elmore Rd and Marshell Rd in Wetumpka.

A brief EF0 tornado touched down near Kellyton along County Rd 62 causing a corridor of timber damage that was most notable near the intersection with AL Hwy 9. The tornado continued northeast crossing US Hwy 280 where additional timber damage occurred. The tornado dissipated after causing additional timber damage on County Rd 50.

Start point of damaging straight line wind swath that caused mostly damage to trees with some uprooted trees and small and large limbs down across this area. Some tree limbs fell onto power lines. Damage to structures was determined to be primarily due to impact by timber. Damage pattern was not divergent or convergent, indicating straight-line wind damage. Winds were estimated to be 40 to 50 mph in the area with a swath of 50 to 60 mph range in the most impacted area.

End point of damaging straight line wind swath that caused mostly damage to trees with some uprooted trees and small and large limbs down across this area. Some tree limbs fell onto power lines. Damage to structures was determined to be primarily due to impact by timber. Damage pattern was not divergent or convergent, indicating straight-line wind damage. Winds were estimated to be 40 to 50 mph in the area with a swath of 50 to 60 mph range in the most impacted area.

Heavy rainfall caused flooding in Birmingham. A few roadways were temporarily impassable.

Heavy rainfall caused a sink hole to open up near the intersection of North Orange Street and Birtt Place in the city of Eufaula. Law enforcement reports that at least portions of North Randolph, West Broad Street, Cherry Street, Colmont and South Eufaula Avenue are experiencing flooding.

Multiple water rescues occurred overnight in the City of Birmingham following heavy rainfall that led to flash flooding. Notable locations where flooding occurred included Center St North, 4th St North, Green Springs Avenue, 6th Ave S, and 9th St S. Village Creek and Valley Creek also quickly rose into Flood Stage between midnight and 2am.
